textRun.content: Ph.D. Student in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Remick Fellow
Bachelor of Arts with Honors, Art,
University of Notre Dame, Notre Dame, IN
Conduct research on how algorithmically mediated platforms shape online community formation, visibility, and belonging
Develop dissertation research on feed-based platforms, social inference, and emergent belonging
Use qualitative, survey, and computational methods to study platformed social life
Analyze platform communities including TikTok and Truth Social
Led project funded by WikiCred to develop novel methods of combating misinformation on YouTube
Conducted contextual-inquiry interviews to evaluate current user wants and needs surrounding credibility
Developed YouTube extension from design to implementation, employing Adobe XD and React JS
Collaborated with cross-disciplinary researchers on a project analyzing the #SaveTheChildren hashtag and movement on Instagram in relation to QAnon
Developed qualitative codebook to analyze over 1,400 applicable social media posts
Developed user experience for artists with visual impairments to navigate canvas-based applications
Conducted background research to use current working methods to design novel accessibility tool
Conveyed key findings and results to a wide audience of company stakeholders

501(c)(3) supporting Advanced Robotics at the University of Washington
Advanced Robotics at the University of Washington (ARUW)

Coordinated team operations, scheduling, competition logistics, and organizational decision-making

ESTEEM Master’s Program, University of Notre Dame
Designed and taught a graduate course for master’s students on generative AI, prompt design, ethics, and AI product development
Developed course materials, assignments, readings, and project structure

Instructor of Record: Elements of Computing 1
Computer Science and Engineering, University of Notre Dame
Served as Instructor of Record for
, an introductory computing course designed for non-major undergraduate students
Taught foundational programming and computational thinking concepts with an emphasis on accessibility, practical application, and building confidence among students new to computing
Designed and led instruction, assignments, and assessments for learners with diverse academic backgrounds and varying levels of prior technical experience
Computer Science and Engineering, University of Notre Dame
Supported undergraduate instruction across two semesters through grading, office hours, and one-on-one student support
Assisted students in developing understanding of core database concepts, query design, and course assignments
Provided regular feedback on coursework and helped troubleshoot student questions about technical material and project expectations
Computer Science and Engineering, University of Notre Dame
Supported instruction through grading, office hours, and individual mentorship for student assignments and projects
Contributed to assignment design and course materials for an undergraduate HCI course
Guided students in applying human-computer interaction concepts to design, evaluation, and research-focused work

Taught two classes, Calculus 2 and Web Development, taking over mid-year. For Calculus 2, continued and completed the curriculum established by the previous instructor. In Web Development, developed a new curriculum focused on addressing real-world problems identified by the students.
St. Catherine of Siena School, Seattle, WA
Designed and taught a robotics elective for middle school students using Lego Mindstorm robots. Focused on programming principles and real-world use cases.
St. Catherine of Siena School, Seattle, WA
Worked alongside the program director to run the after-school care program, working with kindergarten through eighth grade.
Worked with students to determine individual needs and custom learning plans. Topics tutored include: math (algebra through calculus), physics, chemistry, SAT/ACT prep, English, and writing.
